Or Alternatively, Request That Court On Its Own Motion Reconsider Modify Or Claricfy Its November 25, 2008 Order; 5. And Request That The Court Provide Statement Of Decision
Off Calendar Feb-26-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 290868 The Estate of Jessie W. Johansen.

Appearances: Douglas Voorsanger for Debra Dolch (Administrator).

The Court offered an explanation of the eight categories of the extraordinary fees, stated that the services for which the fees were reduced could have been done by either the Administrator or by someone at a lower hourly rate and that some services were duplicative. The Court also stated that there was no trial, that code 656 through 663 did not apply and therefore, the request for a new trial and a statement of decision were denied. Counsel presented oral argument to support his positions. The matters were taken under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-16-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 290868 The Estate of Jessie M. Johansen.

Appearances: Robert Maize, tax Counsel for Debra Dolch. Douglas Voorsanger for Debra Dolch (Administrator). Arthur Park for Kari Nordass (Beneficiary).

The Court stated that it would not rule on the reserves, the amount not being placed in an interest bearing account and the amended tax issue at this time. Mr. Voorsanger stated that many problems would remain for an extended period of time due to a number Beneficiaries that do not reside here, that he would wait for the tax liabilty to expire before disbursement and that it could be up to four years at the longest. Counsel explained the extent of services they provided to support their fee requests. Mr. Voorsanger stated that the tax deadline was at the end of November and requested authority to distribute some of the assets. The Court commended Counsel and stated that the work was very well done. Mr. Park's petition will be granted, but the fees were taken under submission. Mr. Voorsanger's petition was also taken under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.
